content.problemStatement:
Consider an array AAA of length NNN. You know that for all 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N, 0‚â§Ai‚â§1050 \leq A_i \leq 10^50‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§105. We construct an array BBB of length N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 such that, for all 1‚â§i‚â§N‚àí11 \leq i \leq N - 11‚â§i‚â§N‚àí1, Bi=min(Ai,Ai+1)B_i = min(A_i, A_{i + 1})Bi‚Äã=min(Ai‚Äã,Ai+1‚Äã).

You are given the array BBB, you need to find out the total number of distinct arrays AAA which we could have used to construct the given array BBB.

The answer may be large, so you need to find it modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN
The second line of each test case contains N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 space separated integers - the ithi^{th}ith of which is BiB_iBi‚Äã


Output Format
For each testcase(in a new line), output the count of valid possibilities of array AAA modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.
